finding_results.notes: Reduced anti-Caleb signifiers from ~7 to 2 explicit (stain, pen). Removed: (1) 'social miscalibration she would have found charming in a dating profile' framing from line 23 — coffee is now a neutral observation with no contrast positioning; (2) 'dark blond hair that needed cutting' from line 23; (3) 'Adjunct. Which is a fancy word for underpaid' from line 41 — cut entirely, leaving just 'Brooklyn College'; (4) 'He was funny in a way that didn't work too hard' from line 57; (5) 'an opinion about semicolons that she disagreed with' from line 57; (6) 'a wrong opinion about semicolons' from line 59 recap. Added one neutral/slightly-annoying detail at line 23: Adam is too tall for the folding chair, knees angled out and halfway into Nora's space — a physical fact, not positioned as anti-Caleb contrast. Line 59 recap now lists physical impressions (stain, pen clicking, knees) rather than curated-charm signifiers (taught books, semicolons). Final beat at line 61 unchanged — retains tentative, uncertain tone ('Not hope exactly. Not readiness.').

summary: Single finding addressed via four targeted lexical edits. Thinned anti-Caleb signifier density in the Adam bookstore scene from ~7 accumulated contrasts to 2 (pen, stain). Cut the semicolon opinion (two occurrences), adjunct salary detail, 'hair that needed cutting,' 'social miscalibration' dating-profile framing, and 'funny in a way that didn't work too hard.' Replaced hair/miscalibration with one neutral physical detail (too tall for the folding chair, knees in Nora's space) that registers as a body in a room rather than a thematic counterpoint. Line 59 reflection now recaps physical impressions instead of charm-inventory. The encounter reads as tentative and instinctive — Nora gives her number without the text pre-curating reasons for the reader.
